mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates persistent labor income expansion that feeds into consumption and corporate revenue, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:payrolls averaged 200k+ monthly gains through early 2014
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes are negative on average over any 2-year rolling window, or the rank correlation between PAYEMS changes and returns falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.5%, markets reprice the risk of earlier Federal Reserve tightening because labor markets hit the Fed's communicated threshold, raising discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.3% in May 2014, below Fed's erstwhile 6.5% guidance threshold
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE is below 6.5%, or the mean forward return in triggered episodes is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month increase in industrial production and decrease in unemployment captures a non-linear tightening of productive capacity and labor markets that amplifies earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE fails to outperform the sum of individual signals by 2% annualized over a 3-year window, or the interaction term t-statistic falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns only when the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25%, because near-zero discount rates amplify the present value of consumption-driven corporate cash flows.
evidence_refs: fact:Effective fed funds rate at 0.09% as of May 2014
failure_condition: RSAFS changes fail to predict positive 5-day returns with rank IC above 0.04 during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month momentum in industrial production directly proxies aggregate corporate output and capacity utilization; sustained increases predict positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon of 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following positive INDPRO changes are not significantly different from zero over a 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,000 thousand units signals durable construction demand and broad consumer spending spillovers; crossing this threshold predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1072 thousand units as of April 2014
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1000, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative on average, or the hit rate of positive returns is below 50% over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price inflation below 2% removes the threat of premature Federal Reserve tightening, supporting equity multiples and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 236.254 in April 2014 implying subdued inflation
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during months with CPIAUCSL YoY below 2% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Negative month-over-month growth in real retail sales signals a contraction in consumer demand that precedes downward earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS changes are followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rank correlation between RSAFS changes and returns is positive.
